skillName: Use formal methods for subtraction of decimals; complement of a decimal (1 − p)
misconceptionSummary: Students subtract digit by digit in the wrong order — right digit of top minus left digit of bottom.

steps.narration: The student subtracted eight in the ones from five in the tenths — pairs of digits from different columns. Every digit must be subtracted from the digit directly above it in the same column.

visuals.ruleText: Each digit subtracts only from the digit in the same column directly above it
visuals.subText: Align decimal points → pad → then every column pair is clear

steps.narration: After aligning and padding, each column contains exactly one pair of digits — the top number's digit and the bottom number's digit in that place value. Subtract only within the same column.
Work right to left within aligned columns. Hundredths: zero minus five needs a borrow; tenths: two minus seven needs a borrow; ones: seven minus four is three. Answer: three point five five.

visuals.label: Subtract within columns only — right to left, borrowing as needed

steps.narration: Three point five five. Aligning the decimal point guarantees that every column pair is correct before you subtract a single digit.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: subtracting digits from different columns — e.g. ones digit of top with tenths digit of bottom. Align decimal points first so every column pair is unambiguous.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Never subtract across columns. After aligning and padding, each column has one pair of digits. Subtract only within that pair, right to left.
